violations.text: The back door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025
The door of a large chest refrigerator in the ware washing area was observed in poor repair (gasket and interior plastic cover falling off) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2025

-Several ceiling tiles were observed to be stained in the back. -The ceiling in the back and front prep area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Observed blenders being rinsed out in hand sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC discontinued rinsing blenders in hand sink at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Several food items (cheese, ham, sausage, Linguisa, chicken salad, burrito mix, eggs, steak, egg bites, were observed held at (48-55) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Sanitizer solution bucket was observed at (0) parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label between (200ppm-400pm). Staff changed sanitizer bucket to correction concentration (200ppm) during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Cheese, sausages and linguiça were observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Person-in charge moved foods to thaw in refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Observed thermometer located in lowboy in poor repair/need adjustment. Thermometer stuck at 55 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Person-in-charge discarded old thermometer and placed a new thermometer in cold-hold prep unit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Observed cold hold prep unit located in the prep service area not in good repair during time of inspection. Prep unit shall not be used for TCS foods until prep unit temperature is at 41 degrees Fahrenheits or below. PIC place service order for prep unit with eta of 1pm. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025
The door of a large chest refrigerator in the ware washing area was observed in poor repair (gaskets and interior plastic cover falling off). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024
Observed the outside dumpster not covered/closed. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025
Several ceiling tiles were observed to be stained in ware washing area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The back ware-washing and prep area is lacking a hand-washing sink. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 03/08/2024

The hand washing sink in the front service area was observed being used as a dump sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a sheet pan holding bagels. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

Hand washing aids (soap dispenser) was observed at the mop sink. A sink used for FOOD preparation or UTENSIL washing, or a service sink or curbed cleaning facility used for the disposal of mop water or similar wastes, may not be provided with the handwashing aids and devices. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice and took ice machine out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of ice bine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
The door of a large chest freezer was observed in poor repair (gaskets and interior plastic cover fall off). New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit have an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
The ceiling in the back room (above ware-washing and oven) area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
